volumeTitle: Stichus. Trinummus. Truculentus. Tale of a Travelling Bag. Fragments
label: Stichus (Section 5.4.696b - Section 5.6.765)

sections.label: Section 5.4.696b
sections.original: Abi tu sane superior .
sections.translation: Of course you go to the upper place . And , so that you may understand it , I make a division with you on these terms : consider , and take which province you would even like now to take .

sections.label: Section 5.4.699
sections.original: Quid istuc est provinciai ?
sections.translation: But what ’ s your meaning about this province ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.699b
sections.original: Vtrum Fontine an Libero
sections.translation: Whether you would choose to hold the command over the water or over Bacchus .

sections.label: Section 5.4.700b
sections.original: Nimio liquido Libero .
sections.translation: Over Bacchus , most distinctly .

sections.label: Section 5.4.701
sections.original: sed amica mea et tua dum cenat dumque se exórnat , nos volo
sections.translation: But while she who is your mistress and mine is arranging her hair , and bedecking herself , I wish us to have some diversion among ourselves . I appoint you the commander [*] of this feast .

sections.label: Section 5.4.703
sections.original: Nimium lepide in mentem venit : potius quam in subsellio
sections.translation: Very aptly does it suggest itself to your mind .

sections.label: Section 5.4.704
sections.original: cynice hic accipimur quam in lectis .
sections.translation: Wouldn ’ t we be more suitably [*] entertained like Cynics [*] on benches here , than upon couches ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.704b
sections.original: Immo enim nímio hic dulcius .
sections.translation: Aye , but this is far the most pleasant .

sections.label: Section 5.4.705
sections.original: sed interim , stratege noster , cur hic cessat cantharus ?
sections.translation: But , in the meantime , general of ours , why stands this goblet here ? See how many cups [*] we have drunk .

sections.label: Section 5.4.706b
sections.original: Tot quot digiti tibi sunt in manu .
sections.translation: As many as there are fingers on your hand . The Greek song is , Drink either your five cups [*] or your three , but not your four .

sections.label: Section 5.4.708
sections.original: Tibi propino . decumam a fonte tibi tute inde , si sapis .
sections.translation: I pledge you . Do you take for yourself the tenth part from the fountain [*] , if you are wise . Here ’ s luck to you , luck to us ; here ’ s luck to thee , luck to me ; luck to our Stephanium as well .

sections.label: Section 5.4.710
sections.original: Bibe si bibis .
sections.translation: Drink on , if you are drinking .

sections.label: Section 5.4.710b
sections.original: Non mora erit apud me .
sections.translation: There shall be no skulking in me .

sections.label: Section 5.4.710c
sections.original: Edepol convivi sat est ,
sections.translation: But , troth , I ’ ve had enough of the feast ; would but our mistress come here . If she were here , nothing else would be away .

sections.label: Section 5.4.712
sections.original: Lepide hoc actum est . tibi propino cantharum . vinum tu habes .
sections.translation: ’ Tis bravely done . I pledge you in a goblet .

sections.label: Section 5.4.713
sections.original: Nimis vellem aliquid pulpamenti .
sections.translation: Keep your wine ; [*] I ’ d very much like something by way of a relish [*] .

sections.label: Section 5.4.713b
sections.original: Si horum , quae adsunt , paenitet ,
sections.translation: If you are not satisfied with what ’ s here , there ’ s nothing else . Take some water [*] .

sections.label: Section 5.4.714b
sections.original: Melius dicis ; nil moror cuppedia .
sections.translation: You say right ; I care for no dainties .

sections.label: Section 5.4.715
sections.original: bibe , tibicen . age si quid agis , bíbendum hercle hoc est , ne nega .
sections.translation: Drink away , Piper [*] ; drink , if you do drink . I ’ faith , this must be drunk — don ’ t shirk it . ( Holds the goblet to the PIPER .) Why flinch at what you see must be done by you ? Why don ’ t you drink ? Do it , if you are to do it . Take it , I tell you , for the public pays for this . That ’ s not your way to shirk your drink . Take your pipes [*] out of your mouth .

sections.label: Section 5.4.719
sections.original: Vbi illic biberit , vel servato meum modum vel tu dabo . †
sections.translation: When he has drunk , either do you mind my rules [*] , or else I ’ ll give up .

sections.label: Section 5.4.720
sections.original: nolo ego nos † prosumo bibere . nulli rei erimus postea ;
sections.translation: I don ’ t wish us to drink this straight out ; we shall soon be about nothing [*] ; for , by my faith , almost all in a moment , the cask might be turned head downwards [*] .

sections.label: Section 5.4.722
sections.original: Quid igitur ? quamquam gravatus fuisti , non nocuit tamen .
sections.translation: How now ? Although you did make a fuss about it , still it didn ’ t hurt you . Come , Piper , when you ’ ve done drinking , put back your pipes to your lips ; quickly puff out your cheeks , just like a reptile serpent [*] .

sections.label: Section 5.4.725
sections.original: agedum . Stiche , uter démutassit , poculo multabitur .
sections.translation: Come now , Stichus , whichever of the two breaks order , shall be fined a cup .

sections.label: Section 5.4.726
sections.original: Bonum ius dicis . impetrare oportet qui aequom postulat .
sections.translation: You propose a good regulation . You ought to have your way , who only ask what ’ s fair .

sections.label: Section 5.4.727
sections.original: Age ergo observa . si pecassis , multam hic retinebo ilico .
sections.translation: Mind it then ; if you offend , I ’ ll forthwith take the forfeit on the spot .

sections.label: Section 5.4.728
sections.original: Optimum atque aequissimum oras . em tibi hoc primum omnium :
sections.translation: You ask what ’ s quite right and just .

sections.label: Section 5.4.729
sections.original: haec facetiast , amare inter se rivalis duos ,
sections.translation: ’ Tis a droll thing this , for two persons , rivals of each other , to be courting ,

sections.label: Section 5.4.730
sections.original: uno cantharo potare , únum scortum ducere .
sections.translation: to be drinking from one goblet , and to be kissing one wench . ’ Tis worthy of remark this : I am you , you are I ; of one accord are we . With one mistress are we both in love ; when she ’ s with me , still she ’ s with you ; and when she ’ s with you , she ’ s with me as well ; neither of us envies the other .

sections.label: Section 5.4.734
sections.original: Ohe , iam satis , nolo obtaedescat ; alium ludum nunc volo .
sections.translation: Come , come , there ’ s enough of it ; I don ’ t want it overdone to weariness . I ’ d now like some other sport .

sections.label: Section 5.4.735
sections.original: Vin amicam huc evocemus ? ea saltabit .
sections.translation: Should you like us to invite our mistress out ? She shall give us a dance .

sections.label: Section 5.4.735b
sections.original: Censeo .
sections.translation: I agree .

sections.label: Section 5.4.736
sections.original: Mea suavis , amabilis , amoena Stephanium , ad amores tuos
sections.translation: My sweet one , my lovely one , my pleasing one , Stephanium , do come out of doors to your sweethearts ; to me you are quite charming .

sections.label: Section 5.4.737b
sections.original: At enim pulcherrima .
sections.translation: But to me , indeed , most charming .

sections.label: Section 5.4.738
sections.original: Fac nos hilaros hilariores opera atque adventu tuo .
sections.translation: Make us jovial fellows more jovial by your assistance and your company .

sections.label: Section 5.4.739_740
sections.original: Peregre advenientes te expetimus , Stephaniscidium , mel meum ,
sections.translation: Returning from abroad , we want you , dear little Stephanium , my honey , that is , if our lovingness is pleasing to you , if we are acceptable to you .

sections.label: Section 5.5.742
sections.original: Mórem vobis geram , meaé deliciae . nam íta me Venus amoéna amet ,
sections.translation: I ’ ll indulge you , my dears ; but , so may pretty Venus favour me , I should have already come out of doors here together with you , had I not been sprucing myself up for you . For such is the way of woman ,

sections.label: Section 5.5.745
sections.original: bene cum lauta est , tersa ornata ficta est , infecta est tamen ;
sections.translation: when she is well washed , made clean , dressed and tricked out , still is she incomplete ; and a female who is a courtesan much more quickly acquires dislike for herself by sluttishness than always keeps in favour through neatness .

sections.label: Section 5.5.748
sections.original: Nimium lepide fabulata est .
sections.translation: That ’ s very cleverly said .

sections.label: Section 5.5.748b
sections.original: Veneris mera est oratio ,
sections.translation: ’ Tis the genuine language of Venus .

sections.label: Section 5.5.749
sections.original: Sangarine .
sections.translation: Sagarinus .

sections.label: Section 5.5.749b
sections.original: Quid est ?
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 5.5.749c
sections.original: Totus doleo .
sections.translation: I ’ m in pain all over .

sections.label: Section 5.5.749d
sections.original: Totus ? tanto miserior .
sections.translation: All over ? So much the more unfortunate you .

sections.label: Section 5.5.750
sections.original: Vtrubi accumbo ?
sections.translation: Where do I take my place ?

sections.label: Section 5.5.750b
sections.original: Vtrubí tu vis ?
sections.translation: Wherever you please .

sections.label: Section 5.5.750c
sections.original: Cum ambobus volo , nam ambos amo .
sections.translation: I ’ d like with both of you , for I love you both .

sections.label: Section 5.5.751
sections.original: Vapulat peculium , actum est .
sections.translation: Whack go my savings [*] . I ’ m done for ;

sections.label: Section 5.5.751b
sections.original: Fugit hoc libertas caput .
sections.translation: freedom has abandoned this person of mine .

sections.label: Section 5.5.752
sections.original: Date mihi lócum , ubi accumbam , amabo , siquidem placeo .
sections.translation: Prithee , do give me room , where I may take my place , if , indeed , I am agreable . ( She takes her place .)

sections.label: Section 5.5.753
sections.original: Cupio cúm utroque .
sections.translation: Now I do long to be cozy with you both .

sections.label: Section 5.5.753b
sections.original: Ei mihi , bene ego dispereo . quid ais ?
sections.translation: I ’ m ruined utterly . What were you saying ?

sections.label: Section 5.5.753c
sections.original: Quid est ?
sections.translation: Heyday ! What ’ s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 5.5.754
sections.original: Ita me di ament , numquam enim fíet hodie , haec quin saltet tamen .
sections.translation: So may the Gods favour me , it never shall be otherwise this day but that this girl shall have a dance somehow . Come , my love , my sweet , do dance ; I ’ ll dance too .

sections.label: Section 5.5.756
sections.original: Numquam edepol med istoc vinces , quin ego ibidem pruriam .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , you shan ’ t that way get the better of me , but what I ’ ll have a bit of enjoyment , too , that way .

sections.label: Section 5.5.757
sections.original: Siquidem mihi saltandum est , tum vos date bibat tibicini .
sections.translation: Well , if I must dance , do you then give the Piper something to drink .

sections.label: Section 5.5.758
sections.original: Et quidem nóbis .
sections.translation: Aye , and to me .

sections.label: Section 5.5.758b
sections.original: Tene , tibicen , primum ; postidea loci
sections.translation: Piper , you take first : and after that , if you tipple this off , just as has been your wont before to-day , straightway

sections.label: Section 5.5.760
sections.original: lepidam et suavem cantionem aliquam occupito cinaedicam ,
sections.translation: strike up some merry and amorous tune to dance to , by which we may tingle all over from our very finger nails . Pour some water here .

sections.label: Section 5.6.762
sections.original: tene tu hóc , educe . dudum placuit potio :
sections.translation: Take this , you ; toss it off . The drink didn ’ t please him just now ; now at last he takes it with less difficulty . Take it , you . ( To STEPHANIUM .) In the meantime , apple of my eye , give me a kiss while he ’ s drinking .

sections.label: Section 5.6.765
sections.original: Prostibilest tandem ? stantem stanti savium
sections.translation: Why , it ’ s the way of a common strumpet , for a damsel to give a kiss standing to her sweetheart as he stands . [*]
